title: कृष्ण सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कृष्ण सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
अस्य श्री कृष्ण सहस्रनाम स्तोत्र महामन्त्रस्य
पराशर ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः।
श्री कृष्णपरमात्मा देवता। क्लीं बीजम्। कृष्णाय इति शक्तिः।
श्री कृष्ण प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्।
वसुदेवसुतं देवं कंसचाणूरमर्दनम्।
देवकीपरमानन्दं कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्॥१॥

मन्दारमूले मदनाभिरामं
बिम्बाधरापूरितवेणुनादम्।
गोगोपगोपीजनमध्यसंस्थं
गोपालमीशं परिभावयामि॥२॥

कस्तूरीतिलकं ललाटपटले वक्षःस्थले कौस्तुभं
नासाग्रे वरमौक्तिकं करतले वेणुं करे कंकणम्।
सर्वाङ्गे हरिचन्दनं सुललितं कण्ठे च मुक्तावलीं
गोपस्त्रीपरिवेष्टितं विजयते गोपालचूडामणिम्॥३॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥

कृष्णः श्रीवल्लभः शार्ङ्गी विष्वक्सेनो जनार्दनः।
वेदवेद्यो दयानिधिः विधाता श्रीधरो विभुः॥१॥

सनातनस्तनूस्थाणुर्धन्वन्तरिर्दिवाकरः।
मन्दारमालाभरणो मन्दारकुसुमप्रियः॥२॥

गोपालो गोपतिर्गोप्ता गोवर्धनधरो हरिः।
गोविन्दो गोपवेषश्च गोपीजनवल्लभः॥३॥

यशोदानन्दनो बालो नन्दगोपप्रियात्मजः।
धेनुकासुरमर्दी च कालीयफणिनर्दनः॥४॥

कंसारिः कंसहन्ता च केशवो मधुसूदनः।
दैत्यदानवसंहर्ता शंखचक्रगदाधरः॥५॥

वासुदेवो देवकीजो नन्दात्मजो महाबलः।
बालक्रीडो बालमुकुन्दो बालगोपालरूपधृक्॥६॥

पूतनाप्राणहर्ता च शकटासुरभञ्जनः।
तृणावर्तान्तको बाल्ये गोपाङ्गनाविहारकृत्॥७॥

नवनीतहरो बालो माखनचोरो मनोहरः।
दधिमन्थनलीलाढ्यो यशोदाबन्धनो हरिः॥८॥

वृन्दावनचरो विष्णुर्बृन्दावनविहारकृत्।
बर्हापीडो वनमाली मयूरपिच्छधारकः॥९॥

वेणुवादनतत्परो रासलीलाविहारकृत्।
गोपीवस्त्रापहर्ता च गोवर्धनगिरीश्वरः॥१०॥

कालिन्दीतटसंचारी यमुनातीरवासकृत्।
राधाप्रिये राधिकेशो राधामाधव ईश्वरः॥११॥

कुब्जाविमोचनो धीरो मथुरापुरभूषणम्।
उद्धवप्रियसन्देशो विदुरातिथिवत्सलः॥१२॥

द्वारकाधीशो रुक्मिणीरमणो विभुः।
सत्यभामापतिर्लीलामानुषो गोकुलप्रभुः॥१३॥

पार्थसारथिरीशानो गीतामृतमहोदधिः।
धर्मसंस्थापनो दक्षः पार्थप्रियसखो गुरुः॥१४॥

भीष्मपितामहज्ञानदायको भक्तवत्सलः।
दुर्योधनकुलान्तश्च पाण्डवप्रियरक्षकः॥१५॥

कौरवान्तकरो वीरो युद्धशास्त्रविशारदः।
धर्मराजप्रियो जिष्णुरर्जुनात्मा किरीटधृक्॥१६॥

शिशुपालशिरश्छेत्ता दन्तवक्त्रनिषूदनः।
चाणूरमर्दनो मल्लमर्दनो बलसंयुतः॥१७

रुक्मिणीहरणो धीरो जाम्बवत्प्रदसत्पतिः।
स्यमन्तकमणिप्राप्तो सत्राजित्कन्यकापतिः॥१८॥

पुराणपुरुषो देवो ब्रह्मण्यो ब्राह्मणप्रियः।
धर्मगोप्ता धर्मपालो धर्मात्मा धर्मवत्सलः॥१९॥

द्रौपदीमानरक्षी च दुर्वासतृप्तिकारकः।
सुदामाक्षतभोक्ता च अक्षयान्नप्रदो हरिः॥२०॥

महाभारतबीजश्च गीताज्ञानप्रदो गुरुः।
सर्वधर्ममये कृष्णो योगेश्वर ईश्वरः॥२१॥

चतुर्भुजो दीर्घबाहुः श्रीवत्सांकितवक्षसः।
मुरारिर्मोहनो मायो मुकुन्दो मुनिवन्दितः॥२२॥

हृषीकेशो दामोदरो माधवो मधुरद्विपः।
केशवः केशिहन्ता च नारायणोऽच्युतो हरिः॥२३॥

वैकुण्ठनाथो भगवान् श्रीपतिः पुरुषोत्तमः।
श्रीनिवासो जगन्नाथो वासुदेवो जनार्दनः॥२४॥

विश्वरूपो विश्वनाथो विश्वगर्भो विचक्षणः।
नारायणो हृषीकेशो गोविन्दो गरुडध्वजः॥२५॥

सर्वलोकमहेशानो सर्वलोकनमस्कृतः।
सर्वज्ञः सर्वगः शान्तः सर्वात्मा सर्ववन्दितः॥२६॥

परमात्मा परं ब्रह्म सच्चिदानन्दविग्रहः।
आनन्दघनरूपश्च ज्ञानगम्यो निरञ्जनः॥२७॥

वेणुगोपालरूपश्च राधामाधवसुन्दरः।
परब्रह्म स्वरूपश्च नित्यानन्दो जगद्गुरुः॥२८॥

सच्चिदानन्दकन्दश्च गोपवेषधरो मणिः।
योगीश्वरो योगनाथो योगमायासमावृतः॥२९॥

कृष्णः कमलपत्राक्षो वासुदेवो जगत्पतिः।
सर्वदेवमयः श्रीमान् नित्यश्शुद्धो निराकुलः॥३०॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
सहस्रनामैतद्दिव्यं कृष्णस्य परमात्मनः।
यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥

भक्तिं श्रीकृष्णचन्द्रस्य लभते नात्र संशयः।
सर्वकामावाप्तिश्चैव मोक्षश्चान्ते भवेद्ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री महाभारते श्री कृष्ण सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् (ध्यानम् एवं प्रथम ६०+ नामानि) ॥

॥ ॐ क्लीं कृष्णाय नमः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Krishna Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Asya Shri Krishna Sahasranama Stotra Mahamantrasya
Parashara Rishiph. Anushtup Chhandah.
Shri Krishna-paramatma Devata. Klim Bijam. Krishnaya Iti Shaktih.
Shri Krishna Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Krishnam Vande Jagad-gurum.
Vasudeva-sutam Devam Kamsa-Chanura-mardanam.
Devaki-paramanandaam Krishnam Vande Jagad-gurum.||1||

Mandara-mule Madana-abhiramam
Bimba-adhara-apurita-venu-nadam.
Go-gopa-gopi-jana-madhya-samstham
Gopalam-isham Paribhavayami.||2||

Kasturi-tilakam Lalata-patale Vakshah-sthale Kaustubham
Nasagre Vara-mauktikam Karatale Venum Kare Kankanam.
Sarvange Hari-chandanam Su-lalitam Kanthe cha Mukta-avalim
Gopa-stri-pariveshtitam Vijayate Gopala-chudamanim.||3||

|| Stotram ||

Krishnah Shri-vallabhah Sharngi Vishvakseno Janardanah.
Veda-vedyo Dayanidhi Vidhata Shridharo Vibhuh.||1||

Sanatan-stanuh-sthanur-Dhanvantarir-divakarah.
Mandara-mala-abharano Mandara-kusuma-priyah.||2||

Gopalo Gopatir-gopta Govardhana-dharo Harih.
Govindo Gopa-veshah-cha Gopi-jana-vallabhah.||3||

Yashoda-nandano Balo Nanda-gopa-priya-atmajah.
Dhenuka-asura-mardi cha Kaliya-phani-nardanah.||4||

Kamsarih Kamsa-hanta cha Keshavo Madhusudanah.
Daitya-danava-samharta Shankha-chakra-gada-dharah.||5||

Vasudevo Devaki-jo Nanda-atmajo Mahabalah.
Bala-krido Bala-mukundo Bala-gopala-rupa-dhrik.||6||

Putana-prana-harta cha Shakata-asura-bhanjanah.
Trinavarta-antako Balye Gopa-angana-vihara-krit.||7||

Navanita-haro Balo Makhana-choro Manoharah.
Dadhi-manthana-lila-adhyo Yashoda-bandhano Harih.||8||

Vrindavana-charo Vishnur-Vrindavana-vihara-krit.
Barha-pido Vanamali Mayura-pichchha-dharakah.||9||

Venu-vadana-tatparo Rasa-lila-vihara-krit.
Gopi-vastra-apaharta cha Govardhana-giri-ishvarah.||10||

Kalindi-tata-sanchari Yamuna-tira-vasa-krit.
Radha-priye Radhikesho Radha-Madhava Ishvarah.||11||

Kubja-vimochano Dhiro Mathura-pura-bhushanam.
Uddhava-priya-sandesho Vidura-atithi-vatsalah.||12||

Dvaraka-adhisho Rukmini-ramano Vibhuh.
Satyabhama-patir-lila-manusho Gokula-prabhuh.||13||

Partha-sarathir-ishano Gitamrita-mahodadhih.
Dharma-samsthapano Dakshah Partha-priya-sakho Guruh.||14||

Bhishma-pitamaha-jnana-dayako Bhakta-vatsalah.
Duryodhana-kulantash-cha Pandava-priya-rakshakah.||15||

Kaurava-antakaro Viro Yuddha-shastra-visharadah.
Dharma-raja-priyo Jishnur-Arjuna-atma Kiriti-dhrik.||16||

Shishupala-shirash-chhetta Dantavaktra-nishudanah.
Chanura-mardano Malla-mardano Bala-samyutah.||17||

Rukmini-harano Dhiro Jambavat-prada-satpatih.
Symantaka-mani-prapto Satrajit-kanyaka-patih.||18||

Purana-purusho Devo Brahmanyo Brahmana-priyah.
Dharma-gopta Dharma-palo Dharmatma Dharma-vatsalah.||19||

Draupadi-mana-rakshi cha Durvasa-tripthi-karakah.
Sudama-akshata-bhokta cha Akshaya-anna-prado Harih.||20||

Mahabharata-bijash-cha Gita-jnana-prado Guruh.
Sarva-dharma-maye Krishno Yogeshvara Ishvarah.||21||

Chaturbhujo Dirgha-bahuh Shrivatsa-ankita-vakshasah.
Murarih Mohano Mayo Mukundo Muni-vanditah.||22||

Hrishikesho Damodaro Madhavo Madhura-dvipah.
Keshavah Keshi-hanta cha Narayano'chyuto Harih.||23||

Vaikuntha-natho Bhagavan Shripatih Purushottamah.
Shri-nivaso Jagannatho Vasudevo Janardanah.||24||

Vishva-rupo Vishva-natho Vishva-garbho Vichakshanah.
Narayano Hrishikesho Govindo Garuda-dhvajah.||25||

Sarva-loka-maheshano Sarva-loka-namaskritah.
Sarvajnah Sarva-gah Shantah Sarvatma Sarva-vanditah.||26||

Paramatma Param Brahma Sachchidananda-vigrahah.
Ananda-ghana-rupash-cha Jnana-gamyo Niranjanah.||27||

Venu-gopala-rupash-cha Radha-Madhava-sundarah.
Parabrahma Svarupash-cha Nityanando Jagad-guruh.||28||

Sachchidananda-kandash-cha Gopa-vesha-dharo Manih.
Yogishvaro Yoga-natho Yoga-maya-samavritah.||29||

Krishnah Kamala-patrakso Vasudevo Jagat-patih.
Sarva-deva-mayah Shriman Nityash-shuddho Nirakulah.||30||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Sahasra-namaitad-divyam Krishnasya Paramatmanah.
Yah Pathet-pratar-utthaya Sarva-papaih Pramuchyate.

Bhaktim Shri-Krishna-chandrasya Labhate Natra Samshayah.
Sarva-kama-avaptish-chaiva Mokshash-chante Bhaved-dhruvam.

|| Iti Shri Mahabharate Shri Krishna Sahasranama Stotram (Dhyanam evam Prathama 60+ Namani) ||

|| Om Klim Krishnaya Namah ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान — वसुदेव के पुत्र, कंस और चाणूर का मर्दन करने वाले, देवकी के परमानन्द, जगद्गुरु कृष्ण को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ।

मन्दार वृक्ष के नीचे, कामदेव के समान सुन्दर, बिम्बफल जैसे अधरों पर वेणु बजाते, गो-गोप-गोपियों के मध्य विराजमान गोपाल ईश्वर का मैं भावपूर्वक ध्यान करता हूँ।

ललाट पर कस्तूरी तिलक, वक्ष पर कौस्तुभ मणि, नासाग्र पर मोती, करतल में वेणु, कर में कंकण, सर्वांग पर हरिचन्दन, कण्ठ में मुक्तावली, गोपस्त्रियों से परिवेष्टित गोपालचूडामणि की जय हो।

कृष्ण, श्रीवल्लभ, गोपाल, गोवर्धनधर, यशोदानन्दन, बालमुकुन्द, माखनचोर, वृन्दावनचर, वेणुवादक, राधाप्रिय, द्वारकाधीश, पार्थसारथि, गीतामृतमहोदधि — ये कृष्ण सहस्रनाम के दिव्य नाम हैं।

प्रातःकाल इन सहस्रनामों के पाठ से सभी पाप नष्ट होते हैं, कृष्ण भक्ति, सर्वकामनापूर्ति और अन्त में मोक्ष प्राप्त होता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीkrishnasahasranamaमन्त्रस्य पराशर ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री कृष्ण परमात्मा देवता। क्लीं बीजम्। कृष्णाय शक्तिः। श्री कृष्ण प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Supreme hymn containing 1000 names of Lord Krishna covering all His lilas
Covers Bala-gopala, Vrindavana, Mathura, Dvaraka, and Kurukshetra lilas
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and bestows krishna bhakti and spiritual liberation
Invokes all desires and ultimately leads to moksha
Recitation on Janmashtami and Ekadashi yields manifold results
Invokes Krishna as Jagadguru and the speaker of Bhagavad Gita
description: Krishna Sahasranama Stotram (कृष्ण सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names dedicated to Lord Krishna. Rooted in Mahabharata tradition, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to supreme hymn containing 1000 names of Lord Krishna covering all His lilas, cover Bala-gopala, Vrindavana, Mathura, Dvaraka, and Kurukshetra lilas, and recitation on Janmashtami and Ekadashi yields manifold results. It is especially recommended on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami and Holi for maximum spiritual benefit. With 30 verses and a reading time of about 30 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Krishna Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Krishna Sahasranama in Sanskrit with dhyana shlokas and Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Lord Krishna for bhakti, knowledge, and liberation.

seoFaqs.question: Where does the Krishna Sahasranama originate from?
seoFaqs.answer: Multiple recensions of the Krishna Sahasranama exist within the Hindu canon. The most widely known thousand-name lists of Krishna are preserved in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana, the Padma Purana (Uttara Khanda), and the Garga Samhita, where Krishna is praised by sages, gopis, and devas. The Vishnu Sahasranama in the Anushasana Parva of the Mahabharata, recited by Bhishma to Yudhishthira in the presence of Krishna himself, is also widely chanted as a Krishna prayer because Krishna is identified as the supreme Vishnu in Vaishnava tradition. (Source: Wikipedia, Vishnu Sahasranama; Vyasa Online, Anushasana Parva.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Krishna Sahasranama related to the Vishnu Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Sahasranama, found in chapter 149 of the Anushasana Parva of the Mahabharata, contains 108 shlokas in Anushtup metre and lists 1000 names of Vishnu, of whom Krishna is regarded as the purna avatara. Krishna himself is present when Bhishma narrates these names to Yudhishthira from his bed of arrows. The Krishna Sahasranama from the Puranas focuses specifically on Krishna's lila in Vrindavan, Mathura, and Dwaraka, naming his pastimes, companions, and divine qualities, while the Vishnu Sahasranama covers the broader Vaishnava theology of the supreme Lord. (Source: Wikipedia, Vishnu Sahasranama; Sacred Texts Archive.)

seoFaqs.question: Who narrates the Sahasranama in the Mahabharata?
seoFaqs.answer: In the Anushasana Parva (Book 13) of the Mahabharata, the dying Bhishma Pitamah, lying on the bed of arrows after the Kurukshetra war, narrates the Sahasranama to Yudhishthira, the eldest Pandava, who is burdened with grief over the deaths of his kinsmen. Krishna is present during this discourse, and the sage Vyasa later records the teaching. Bhishma instructs Yudhishthira that recitation of these thousand names removes fear, sorrow, and sin, and grants liberation. (Source: Quora summary citing Anushasana Parva; Sacred Texts Archive, m13a014.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Krishna Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Vaishnava practice recommends reciting the Krishna Sahasranama on Ekadashi (the 11th lunar day), Janmashtami (Krishna's appearance day in Bhadrapada), Rohini Nakshatra days, and during the holy month of Kartik. Daily recitation at brahma muhurta (pre-dawn) before a Krishna image, with offerings of tulsi leaves, is considered ideal. Many devotees also chant it during the four-month Chaturmasya vow and on Wednesdays, the day traditionally associated with Krishna in the planetary scheme. (Source: Naam Seva; Hindutsav.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees seek from this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti (fruit-declaration) verses of the Sahasranama state that sincere recitation grants protection from fear, disease, and untimely death, removes obstacles in dharmic life, fulfils material needs in moderation, and ultimately leads to bhakti and moksha. Bhishma tells Yudhishthira that one who recites these names with devotion is freed from sorrow. In the Krishna-bhakti tradition, the stotra is also valued as a meditation on Krishna's lila, fostering rasa (devotional emotion) rather than mere ritual benefit. (Source: Vyasa Online, Anushasana Parva; Naam Seva.)
